name: make-automation
description: Automate Make (Integromat) tasks via Rube MCP (Composio): operations, enums, language and timezone lookups. Always search tools first for current schemas.
capabilities: Retrieve Make scenario operation logs and usage data via MAKE_GET_OPERATIONS
List all supported language codes for Make scenarios via MAKE_LIST_ENUMS_LANGUAGES
List all supported timezone identifiers for scheduling via MAKE_LIST_ENUMS_TIMEZONES
Validate language and timezone values before scenario configuration
Analyze operation success and failure rates for scenario monitoring
Compose multi-tool workflows by connecting multiple app toolkits
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 36 static analyzer findings are false positives. The external_commands detections (22 locations) are Markdown backticks used for code formatting around MCP tool names, not shell execution. The network finding is a documentation URL for MCP server setup. The cryptographic algorithm findings incorrectly matched 'MAKE' in tool names like MAKE_GET_OPERATIONS. This is documentation-only content with no executable code.
valueStatement: Managing Make (Integromat) automation requires tracking operations and validating configuration values. This skill provides direct access to Make operations data and enum lookups through Composio's Make toolkit via Rube MCP.
limitations: The Make toolkit in Composio has limited tools (operations, languages, timezones only)
Cannot create, edit, or directly trigger Make scenarios through this toolkit
For full scenario management, use Make's native API or webhook endpoints directly
Operations data may be paginated and require date range filtering for large datasets

useCases.targetUser: Automation engineers managing production Make workflows
useCases.title: Scenario Operations Monitoring
useCases.description: Monitor Make scenario health by retrieving operation logs, calculating success and failure ratios, and identifying scenarios with high error rates for troubleshooting.

useCases.title: Configuration Validation
useCases.description: Validate language codes and timezone identifiers before configuring Make scenarios to prevent configuration errors and ensure correct locale and scheduling settings.

useCases.targetUser: Teams migrating from Make to direct API integrations
useCases.title: Multi-App Workflow Composition
useCases.description: Build equivalent automation workflows by composing individual app toolkits instead of relying on Make, using RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS to discover available tools and RUBE_CREATE_UPDATE_RECIPE for reusable workflows.

promptTemplates.title: Check Make connection status
promptTemplates.prompt: Check if my Make connection is active via RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit make. If not active, help me complete the authentication flow.
promptTemplates.scenario: Initial setup - verifying Make connection before running operations

promptTemplates.title: Get recent operation logs
promptTemplates.prompt: Retrieve Make operations from the last 7 days using MAKE_GET_OPERATIONS. Show me which scenarios have the highest failure rates and summarize any error patterns.
promptTemplates.scenario: Weekly scenario health monitoring and error analysis

promptTemplates.title: Validate timezone for scheduling
promptTemplates.prompt: I need to set a scenario schedule for Pacific Time. Call MAKE_LIST_ENUMS_TIMEZONES and confirm the correct timezone identifier string I should use.
promptTemplates.scenario: Configuring scenario schedules with correct timezone values

promptTemplates.title: Build equivalent workflow without Make
promptTemplates.prompt: My Make scenario connects Google Sheets to Slack. Search for available Google Sheets and Slack tools via RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS, then help me compose an equivalent direct workflow using those toolkits instead of Make.
promptTemplates.scenario: Migrating from Make to direct app toolkit integrations

bestPractices: Always call RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS first to get current tool schemas before executing any operations
Cache language and timezone enum results locally since they are static and rarely change
Filter MAKE_GET_OPERATIONS calls by date range to avoid fetching excessive data and hitting rate limits
antiPatterns: Using hardcoded language codes or timezone strings without validating against enum endpoints
Making rapid repeated calls to the same endpoint without caching results
Fetching all operations data without date range filters, causing slow responses and quota issues

faq.question: How do I set up the Make connection for this skill?
faq.answer: Add https://rube.app/mcp as an MCP server in your client configuration. Then call RUBE_MANAGE_CONNECTIONS with toolkit make. If not active, follow the returned auth link to complete Make authentication.

faq.question: Can I create or trigger Make scenarios with this skill?
faq.answer: No. The Composio Make toolkit currently only supports reading operations data and enum lists. For creating or triggering scenarios, use Make's native API or webhook endpoints directly.

faq.question: How often do the language and timezone lists change?
faq.answer: These enum lists are static and rarely change. Cache the results at workflow start and refresh only when starting a new session.

faq.question: Why am I getting pagination tokens in operations responses?
faq.answer: Active Make accounts may have large volumes of operation records. Use date range filters in MAKE_GET_OPERATIONS to limit result sets and handle pagination tokens when present.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to replace my Make workflows entirely?
faq.answer: Not directly through the Make toolkit. However, you can compose equivalent workflows by searching for individual app toolkits via RUBE_SEARCH_TOOLS and building step-by-step workflows using RUBE_CREATE_UPDATE_RECIPE.

faq.question: What should I do if operations data shows high failure rates?
faq.answer: Investigate the error messages in failed operations. Common causes include expired authentication tokens, rate limit exceeded, or target API changes. Check that your Make connection is active and has required permissions.
